architecture_summary: ViT-22B is a Transformer-based encoder model that resembles the original Vision Transformer but adds parallel Attention-MLP blocks, query/key normalization, and omitted biases to improve efficiency and training stability at scale.

novel_contributions: We present ViT-22B, the largest dense ViT model to date.
We uncover pathological training instabilities which prevent scaling the default recipe, and demonstrate architectural changes which make it possible.
We carefully engineer the model to enable model-parallel training at unprecedented efficiency.
ViT-22B demonstrates increasing performance with scale on downstream tasks, often with a lightweight linear model on frozen features.
We observe improved tradeoff between fairness and performance, state-of-the-art alignment to human visual perception in terms of shape/texture bias, and improved robustness.

key_claims.claim: The paper presents ViT-22B, a 22B-parameter ViT and the largest dense ViT model to date.
ViT-22B requires architectural changes such as parallel layers, QK normalization, and omitted biases to train stably at scale.
The model is trained on a version of JFT extended to around 4B images, for 177k steps with batch size of 65k.
ViT-22B achieves 89.5% on ImageNet with frozen features, and 85.9% zero-shot ImageNet accuracy with a LiT text tower.
key_claims.evidence_section: Abstract; 4.2.1 Linear probing; 4.2.2 Zero-shot via locked-image tuning

key_claims.claim: The paper reports that ViT-22B sets the new SOTA on ObjectNet in zero-shot transfer.
ViT-22B transfers better to dense prediction and video classification than smaller ViT backbones in the reported settings.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.3 Transfer to dense prediction; 4.4 Transfer to video classification

key_claims.claim: ViT-22B improves fairness, calibration, robustness, and human alignment, including a previously unseen 87% shape bias.
Distilling ViT-22B into ViT-B/16 and ViT-L/16 yields new SOTA on both sizes.
